recent_coach_change: Popovic took over in September 2024, replacing Graham Arnold; led Australia on an eight-game unbeaten run to direct qualification. No change since.
base_formation: Balanced structure built around a veteran spine; back four with experienced GK Mathew Ryan
qualification_path: Qualified directly via AFC third round (Group C runners-up), reaching the World Cup without a playoff for the first time since 2014.
key_players: Mathew Ryan (GK) — captain, 104 caps, experienced leader anchoring the defense
Jackson Irvine (33, 81 caps) — midfield leader and energy
Harry Souttar — returning centre-back providing aerial presence
Nestory Irankunda (Watford) — rising 20-year-old winger with pace and shooting power
Mohamed Toure (Norwich) — young striker, nine Championship goals

injuries_doubts.issue: hamstring injury suffered in Middlesbrough's Championship play-off final defeat to Hull City

squad_notes: Popovic named his 26-man squad on June 1, 2026 after an extended camp in Sarasota, Florida (running since April), rotating eight players in and eight out. Cristian Volpato (Sassuolo) was a notable inclusion after switching allegiance from Italy; Tete Yengi was also a first-time call-up. Harry Souttar and Matthew Leckie returned from long absences, as did Jackson Irvine, Mohamed Toure and Cameron Devlin. Veteran Martin Boyle was omitted. 17 players set for World Cup debuts.
warmup_assessment: Underwhelming final tune-up. Australia lost 1-0 to Mexico in their last friendly on June 2, 2026. Leckie's appearance in that match was his first since June 2024 following hip surgery in December 2025.
shape_verdict: An experienced, well-drilled but goal-light Socceroos side leaning on a veteran spine and youthful wide threats. Losing creator Riley McGree to injury on the eve of the tournament dents their attacking depth.

news.headline: Tony Popovic names 26-man Socceroos World Cup squad; Volpato switches from Italy, Boyle omitted
Australia fall 1-0 to Mexico in final pre-World Cup friendly
Riley McGree suffers hamstring injury in Middlesbrough play-off final, ruled out of World Cup
Socceroos squad notes: two potential debutants, five returnees for World Cup 2026
